questionText: Read the following instructions: 'Press the power button to turn on the console. Then, insert the game disc into the drive. Wait for the game to load.' What is the purpose of this text?
options: To describe the history of video games.
To give a narrative about a gaming experience.
To provide a set of instructions on how to start a console game.
To argue against playing video games.
explanation: The text gives a sequence of actions to perform, which is a set of instructions. It does not describe history, tell a story, or argue a point.

questionText: Which of the following sentences best describes a 'computer game'?
options: A game played on a mobile phone.
An electronic game that involves interaction with a user interface to generate visual feedback on a computing device.
A board game played with friends.
A physical sport played outdoors.
explanation: By definition, a computer game is an electronic game that involves interaction with a user interface to generate visual feedback on a computing device (like a PC).

questionText: Identify the sentence with the correct use of 'neither... nor'.
options: Neither the game nor the console is working.
Neither the game nor the console are working.
Neither the game or the console is working.
Neither the game nor the console were working.
explanation: When using 'neither... nor', the verb agrees with the subject closest to it. In this case, 'console' is singular, so the verb should be singular ('is'). The other options contain grammatical errors in agreement or structure.

questionText: In the sentence, 'You can either play the racing game or the adventure game, but you can also watch a movie,' what is the main idea?
options: The person only has two options.
The person has a choice between two specific games, with a third unrelated option.
The person must play both games.
The person is being forced to play the racing game.
explanation: The structure 'either... or' presents a choice between two things (the racing game or the adventure game). The word 'also' introduces an additional, separate option (watching a movie).

questionText: Which of the following is a phrasal verb commonly used in the context of video games?
options: Turn on
Look after
Break down
Give up
explanation: A phrasal verb is a verb combined with a preposition or adverb. While many are common, 'turn on' is frequently used with electronic devices like computers and consoles. 'Look after', 'break down', and 'give up' are less specific to this context.

questionText: What does it mean when a player says, 'I will log in to my gaming account'?
options: They will create a new account.
They will sign out of their account.
They will access their account by entering their username and password.
They will delete their account.
explanation: To 'log in' is a phrasal verb meaning to gain access to a computer system or online service by entering your username and password.

options: Although the game is difficult, but I enjoy playing it.
Although the game is difficult, I enjoy playing it.
Although the game is difficult I enjoy playing it, but.
I enjoy playing it, although but the game is difficult.
explanation: 'Although' is a subordinating conjunction used to introduce a contrast. The correct structure is 'Although + clause, + main clause'. Option 1 is incorrect because it adds a second conjunction ('but'). Option 2 is the only grammatically correct sentence.

questionText: Write a short paragraph about your favorite video game. Which of the following sentences would be the best topic sentence?
options: The graphics are very realistic.
I play on my Xbox.
My favorite video game is 'The Legend of Zelda' because it has an engaging story.
Many people enjoy playing video games.
explanation: A good topic sentence introduces the main idea of the paragraph. Option 3 clearly states the subject (favorite video game) and gives a reason, making it the most effective topic sentence to build a paragraph around.

questionText: Read this short text: 'Playing video games can improve hand-eye coordination. However, it is important to take breaks to rest your eyes. Furthermore, spending too much time on games can lead to a sedentary lifestyle.' What is the main idea of the text?
options: Video games are a waste of time.
Playing video games has both advantages and disadvantages.
Everyone should play video games for their health.
The only problem with video games is eye strain.
explanation: The text presents a balanced view. It first mentions a benefit (improved coordination) and then presents drawbacks (eye strain, sedentary lifestyle) using transition words like 'however' and 'furthermore'. This shows both sides of the topic.

questionText: In the sentence, 'I had never played such an exciting game before,' what does the word 'never' indicate?
options: I played it many times.
It was the first time I played a game like that.
I played it once.
I didn't enjoy it.
explanation: The word 'never' means 'not at any time in the past'. In this context, it indicates that up to that point in time, the speaker had not had such an exciting experience with a game. This was the first such experience.
